title: Scripts Object
description: API object that provides equivalent functionality of the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog. Provides access to the scripts and add-ins that Fusion is aware of. It also supports loading other unknown scripts and add-ins, and creating new scripts and add-ins.

methods.name: addExisting
methods.description: Fusion looks in specific folders for scripts and add-ins, but you can manually add other scripts and add-ins to the list of known scripts and add-ins so they will be listed in the "Scripts and Add-ins" dialog. This method does that.

methods.name: addNew
methods.description: Creates a new script or add-in. This uses the same internal template that's used when creating a new script or add-in using the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog. The provided ScriptInput object defines the information needed to create a new script or add-in.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createScriptInput
methods.description: Creates a new ScriptInput object. Logically, this object is equivalent to the dialog that is shown when you click the "Create" button in the "Scripts and Add-Ins" command dialog. It collects the information needed to create a new script or add-in. To create the script or add-in, call the addNew method, passing in the ScriptInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified script or add-in using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByPath
methods.description: Function that returns the script that is located in the specified folder.

methods.name: itemsByName
methods.description: Function that returns an array of scripts that have the specified name. In most cases this will return an array containing a single script, but it's possible to have more than one script with the same name in the case where the scripts are in different folders.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of scripts and add-ins.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addExisting.title: Scripts.addExisting Method
addExisting.description: Fusion looks in specific folders for scripts and add-ins, but you can manually add other scripts and add-ins to the list of known scripts and add-ins so they will be listed in the "Scripts and Add-ins" dialog. This method does that.
addExisting.return_description: Returns the Script object that represents the script or add-in just added. Returns null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: scriptFolderPath
parameters.description: The full path to the folder that contains the script or add-in.

addNew.title: Scripts.addNew Method
addNew.description: Creates a new script or add-in. This uses the same internal template that's used when creating a new script or add-in using the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog. The provided ScriptInput object defines the information needed to create a new script or add-in.
addNew.return_description: Returns the newly created Script object.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A ScriptInput object which defines the required information to create a new script or add-in. It is created using the createNewScriptInput method.

classType.title: Scripts.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createScriptInput.title: Scripts.createScriptInput Method
createScriptInput.description: Creates a new ScriptInput object. Logically, this object is equivalent to the dialog that is shown when you click the "Create" button in the "Scripts and Add-Ins" command dialog. It collects the information needed to create a new script or add-in. To create the script or add-in, call the addNew method, passing in the ScriptInput object.
createScriptInput.return_description: Returns a ScriptInput object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the script or add-in to create. By default, it will be created in the folder specified by the "Default Path for Scripts and Add-Ins" preference, but a different path can be specified using the returned ScriptInput object. Regardless of where it is created, the name must be unique with respect to the other scripts and add-ins in that folder. If it's not unique the creation of the script or add-in will fail.

parameters.name: programmingLanguage
parameters.description: The programming language to use for the new script or add-in.

parameters.name: isAddIn
parameters.description: Specifies if a script or add-in is to be created. If true, an add-in is created.

item.title: Scripts.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified script or add-in using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByPath.title: Scripts.itemByPath Method
itemByPath.description: Function that returns the script that is located in the specified folder.
itemByPath.return_description: Returns the specified script or null if there isn't a script at the specified path.

parameters.name: folderPath
parameters.description: The full path to the folder that contains the script. This does not include the name of the script, but only the path. For example "C:\Scripts\MyScript" is valid where "C:\Scripts\MyScripts\MyScript.py" is not.

itemsByName.title: Scripts.itemsByName Method
itemsByName.description: Function that returns an array of scripts that have the specified name. In most cases this will return an array containing a single script, but it's possible to have more than one script with the same name in the case where the scripts are in different folders.
itemsByName.return_description: Returns the scripts with the specified name or an empty array if there aren't any scripts with that name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The script name to search for.

count.title: Scripts.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of scripts and add-ins.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = scripts_var.count

isValid.title: Scripts.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = scripts_var.isValid

objectType.title: Scripts.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = scripts_var.objectType
